Package microui.core.style

Class LerpedLoopColor

java.lang.Object
microui.core.style.AbstractColor
microui.core.style.AbstractLerpedColor
microui.core.style.LerpedLoopColor
public class LerpedLoopColor extends AbstractLerpedColor
A continuously looping interpolated color that automatically cycles between start and end colors.

This class extends AbstractLerpedColor with built-in looping behavior. When loop mode is enabled, the color automatically interpolates back and forth between the start and end colors, creating a continuous pulsing or breathing effect.

The default animation speed is set to 0.01 (1% progress per update), which creates a smooth, gradual transition suitable for visual effects.

Looping can be temporarily disabled while keeping the current progress state, allowing for controlled pauses in the animation.

See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • LerpedLoopColor

      public LerpedLoopColor(AbstractColor start, AbstractColor end)
      Constructs a new looping interpolated color with the specified start and end colors. Loop mode is enabled by default with an animation speed of 0.01.
      Parameters:
      start - the starting color of the loop sequence, cannot be null
      end - the ending color of the loop sequence, cannot be null

  • Method Details

    • isLoopEnabled

      public final boolean isLoopEnabled()
      Checks whether loop mode is currently enabled.
      Returns:
      true if the color is automatically cycling between start and end colors, false if the animation is paused at its current progress

    • setLoopEnabled

      public final void setLoopEnabled(boolean loopEnabled)
      Enables or disables loop mode. When enabled, the animation automatically reverses direction when reaching either endpoint, creating a continuous back-and-forth transition. When disabled, the animation stops at the current progress until loop mode is re-enabled.
      Parameters:
      loopEnabled - true to enable continuous looping, false to pause at current progress
